title: Citric acid descaling solution
subtitle: A simple acid solution for removing limescale from kettles and coffee machines
excerpt: A citric acid and water solution that dissolves limescale deposits in kettles, coffee machines, and showerheads. Food-grade citric acid is safer and less corrosive than white vinegar for descaling appliances, and leaves no smell.
sourceNotes: Citric acid for descaling: citric acid (pH around 2 in solution) dissolves calcium carbonate (limescale) through an acid-base reaction forming soluble calcium citrate. This is the same principle as commercial descalers. Food-grade citric acid is safe for use in appliances that contact food and drink. Reference: standard chemistry of acid-carbonate reactions.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 litre of descaling solution (sufficient for 1 kettle or 1 coffee machine cycle)
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make fresh for each descaling session. Citric acid solution has an indefinite shelf life in a sealed container but is most convenient made fresh.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Unused citric acid solution keeps 1 month in a sealed container. Citric acid powder stores indefinitely in a cool dry place.

glossaryTerms.definition: Hard calcium carbonate deposits that form inside appliances, pipes, and around taps where hard water evaporates. Citric acid dissolves limescale through an acid-base reaction that converts insoluble calcium carbonate into soluble calcium citrate.

content.text: through a simple acid reaction: the acid converts insoluble calcium carbonate into soluble calcium citrate, which rinses away in water. At a 2% solution (20 g per litre), this is a gentle, food-safe descaler with no residual smell. It is better than white vinegar for appliances used in food preparation because it leaves no flavour. Most commercial descaling tablets are citric acid with a colourant. The method works the same way for kettles, coffee machines, steam irons, and showerheads.

items.prepNote: 1 litre; tap water will work but distilled gives a consistent result

content.text: Dissolve 20 g of citric acid in 1 litre of water and pour into the kettle. Boil, then leave for 30 minutes. Empty the kettle and rinse 3 times with fresh water before use. For heavy scale, a second cycle may be needed.

Check the machine's manual before use; most manufacturers recommend a dedicated descaler but citric acid is compatible with most drip-filter, percolator, and pod machines. Dissolve 20 g of citric acid in 1 litre of water. Add to the water reservoir and run a full descale cycle (or 2 to 3 brew cycles without coffee). Run 3 full water-only cycles to rinse completely before brewing coffee.

Mix a stronger solution: 50 g of citric acid in 500 ml of warm water. Remove the showerhead or fill a small bag with the solution and attach it around the showerhead with an elastic band so the nozzle is submerged. Leave for 30 to 60 minutes, then rinse thoroughly.

Safe for stainless steel, chrome, most plastic, and glass. Do not use on aluminium surfaces (citric acid can corrode aluminium over time) or on natural stone. Not suitable for espresso machines with aluminium boilers; check the boiler material before use.
Citric acid powder stores indefinitely in a cool dry place in a sealed container. Mixed solution keeps 1 month sealed. Make fresh for each descaling session for the most convenient result.
